description: Wandering motion, screen layout, stat visibility, and nearby (watch-to-watch) battle. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md

userStories.title: The egg hatches after 5 minutes only
userStories.description: As a player, I want my Digitama to hatch on a simple, predictable 5-minute timer so hatching is not sped up by walking or energy. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-314.
acceptanceCriteria: EggHatcher.hatchTarget hatches a Digitama on the 5-minute wall-clock condition ALONE: now - stageEnteredAt >= EggHatcher.maximumEggDuration (5*60). The 500-step condition (stepsToHatch / walkedIt) is removed AND the total-energy condition (earnedIt, the >= edge.minEnergy check) is removed

EggHatcher.stepsToHatch is deleted; any now-dead step/energy parameters in hatchTarget's signature are removed rather than left present-but-unused (grep the callers in MainScreenModel / BackgroundRefresh and update them)

The hatch is still evaluated inside refresh() and BackgroundRefresh, so an app closed on an egg and reopened six minutes later still finds a hatched Baby I; a frozen egg still does not age toward the timer (Freeze.shiftTimeline behaviour unchanged)
The 5-minute check stays >= against stageEnteredAt with the clock injected (now passed in) — no Date() inside hatchTarget, so tests never wait

EggHatcherTests and any suite that previously hatched an egg via energy or steps are rewritten to the time-only rule: assert an egg does NOT hatch at 50 energy and does NOT hatch at 500 steps, and DOES hatch at exactly now == stageEnteredAt + 5 minutes and NOT at 4m59s. Fix, do not skip; note in the story if a test was genuinely about a removed path
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator: a fresh save's egg hatches at ~5 minutes with no health data present, showing the ceremony

userStories.title: A wild encounter during the egg stage auto-flees silently
userStories.description: As a player, while I am still raising an egg, I do not want a BATTLE/FLEE dialog I cannot act on — the egg should just avoid the fight and lose the map steps, with no animation it cannot play. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-315.
acceptanceCriteria: When the active Digimon is an egg (isEgg == true), checkForWildEncounter does NOT set pendingWildEncounter; instead the due encounter is auto-fled

The auto-flee applies the same map-step penalty a normal flee does — reduceRecorded(steps: wildEncounterStepInterval, forMap:) and setEncounterMarker to the new recorded total — and saves via store, so the counter behaves exactly as a manual flee would
No refuse/recoil pose and no 'Fled!' message is shown in the egg path (an egg has no such frames): the show(.pose(.refuse), motion: .recoil, ...) call is skipped

Whether the wild Digimon is recorded as met in the egg path is decided one way and stated in notes (either record it as the non-egg surface path does, or skip the encounter before recordMet) — pick the simpler consistent option
A non-egg Digimon's wild-encounter flow is unchanged: it still presents the BATTLE/FLEE dialog

Tests: with an egg active and an encounter due, checkForWildEncounter leaves pendingWildEncounter nil, applies the step penalty and marker move, and shows no refuse pose; with a non-egg active and the same trigger, pendingWildEncounter is set
Build green, tests pass

userStories.title: Wandering motion advances in discrete steps with randomised reversals
userStories.description: As a player, I want my Digimon to wander in step-by-step moves that do not always go wall-to-wall — sometimes it walks a few steps toward the middle and turns back — so it feels alive rather than mechanical. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-301.
acceptanceCriteria: MovementModel no longer reverses ONLY at ±bound: it may choose a reversal at an interior position, producing runs of a few steps one way then a few the other

Reversal decisions are driven only by the injected seed (deterministic): the same seed plus the same sequence of advance(to:) dates produces the exact same path. No Date() and no .random inside advance(to:)

Movement is still integrated in whole MovementModel.step (0.25s) units, constant pointsPerSecond speed, and maximumCatchUpSteps catch-up is preserved (a long gap is forgiven, not paid back as a sprint)
offset stays clamped to -bound...bound at all times; shrinking bound still pulls a stranded sprite back in
A view redrawing at any cadence walks the same path — two 0.5s advances land where four 0.25s advances do
Tests: a known seed produces at least one interior reversal within N steps; two different seeds produce different paths; the determinism property (repeat run == first run) holds
Build green, tests pass

userStories.title: The Digimon breaks its walk with brief expressions
userStories.description: As a player, I want my walking Digimon to occasionally show a mood — anger, happiness — so it has personality beyond pacing. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-302.
acceptanceCriteria: While wandering, the sprite occasionally plays a short non-walk animation (at minimum angry, frame 8; may include happy, frame 7) for a brief beat, then returns to walking. Drawn with .interpolation(.none)
The choice of when and which expression is deterministic given the injected seed/clock — a test reproduces it without waiting real time; no wall-clock randomness in the model layer
Expressions never fire while the Digimon is sleeping, eating, sick, dead, an unhatched egg, or behind an overlay — they ride only on the ordinary wandering state (isWandering == true)
The expression does not move the sprite's position — the walk resumes from where it stood
A DEBUG launch flag following the existing -*Demo pattern in ContentView stages the expression so it is screenshottable
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing the expression frame mid-wander

userStories.title: A Zz sleep indicator animates in the top-right while sleeping
userStories.description: As a player, I want a clear Zz sign near the top-right of the screen while my Digimon is asleep, so I can tell at a glance it is sleeping. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-316.
acceptanceCriteria: While model.isAsleep == true, a Zz indicator (emoji or drawn 'Zz') shows in the top-right of the main screen with padding from the edges
It is animated (a gentle pulse/rise/fade loop), reading as active rather than a static glyph
It appears only while asleep (absent when awake) and does not overlap or fight the top-right Settings gear — offset/padded so both are legible, or placed clear of the toolbar
It is drawn as an overlay (like the sick badge) and does not shift the layout of the sprite, stats strip or action rows
It does not appear over full-screen overlays (ceremony, battle, memorial, training) — those still cover it
A -sleepDemo-style launch flag stages the asleep state so it is screenshottable
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing the animated top-right Zz while asleep

userStories.title: Map-step and Zz bars return as 2px dash bars under the map
userStories.description: As a player, I want the map-progress and sleep readings shown as thin bars under the map background again, so I can read them without decoding a ring around a button. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-303.
acceptanceCriteria: Two DashBars are drawn under the map background inside the play-area band: one for map-progress (recordedSteps / totalSteps from MapStrip, green) and one for sleep (sleepHours / sleepHoursCap, indigo)
Each bar's height is 2 points (DashBar dashHeight: 2)
The bars sit beneath the map background visually (under the Digimon's play area), not inside the action grid
Bars degrade gracefully when there is no map selected (0 of 0 draws nothing, as the ring did) and before the first layout pass has measured the slot
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing both 2px bars under the map

userStories.title: Remove the dash-ring border from the Map and Sleep buttons
userStories.description: As a player, I want the Map and Sleep buttons to be plain circles like the others, since their readings now live under the map. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-304.
acceptanceCriteria: The DashRing around the Map button and the DashRing around the Sleep button are removed in ActionControls; both render as plain ActionButtonFace circles
No other button loses or gains a ring (Feed/Train/Battle/Clean rings, where present, are untouched)
The map-step and sleep values are no longer passed into the ring for these two buttons; dead parameters are cleaned up (or kept only where US-303's bars still use them)
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing plain Map and Sleep buttons

userStories.title: Light button moves to the top-left toolbar, styled like the gear
userStories.description: As a player, I want the room-light control in the top-left corner, matching the Settings gear in the top-right, so both utility controls sit in the toolbar. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-307.
acceptanceCriteria: The Light control is removed from the action grid and added as a ToolbarItem at .topBarLeading in ContentView
Its icon style matches the Settings gear (plain SF Symbol Image, same weight, no filled-circle background), e.g. lightbulb / lightbulb.fill reflecting the current lightState
Tapping it still cycles the light exactly as model.cycleLight() did from the grid button
It carries an accessibility label (like the gear's 'Settings')
The action grid no longer contains a Light button
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing the top-left Light and top-right gear

userStories.title: Action buttons re-chunk into rows of 4 / 3 / 4
userStories.description: As a player, I want the action buttons laid out in rows of 4, then 3, then 4, so the grid is balanced and easy to scan. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-305.
acceptanceCriteria: ActionGridLayout (or its replacement) lays the buttons out as row 1 = 4, row 2 = 3, row 3 = 4, using the button set AFTER Light left the grid (US-307). Confirm and document the exact count; if it is not exactly 11, keep the 4/3/4 intent and let the final row absorb the remainder, stating the count in notes
Buttons stay within ActionButtonFace.diameter sizing rules and do not clip on the narrowest supported screen (176pt wide, 41mm)
A pure layout test asserts the row split (4/3/4) and that every button is present exactly once
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing the 4/3/4 rows

userStories.title: The main screen scrolls
userStories.description: As a player, I want to scroll the whole main screen so the action buttons come up into easy reach for tapping. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-306.
acceptanceCriteria: The main digimon content in ContentView is wrapped so the play area + stats + action rows scroll vertically when they do not all fit, bringing the buttons closer to the thumb
Scrolling does not break the map background / scrim / poop anchoring (SpriteSlotBoundsKey): the map, dim scrim and mess still align to the play-area band after layout
Overlays (ceremony, battle, wild encounter, memorial, training) still cover the full screen and are unaffected by the scroll
The Digimon's play area still gets a sensible size and does not collapse to nothing inside the scroll view
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot showing the scrolled-down state with buttons in reach

userStories.title: Party rows show current HP / ATK / AGI as dash bars
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see each Digimon's current battle stats in the Party list, so I can compare my roster at a glance. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-308.
acceptanceCriteria: Each PartyRow carries its current HP/ATK/AGI values (current = effectiveStat, i.e. base + trained bonus), resolved without a view in PartyRow.rows(...), so it is testable
The party row view draws three compact dash bars (HP red, ATK orange, AGI green) using the existing BattleStat symbols/tints, showing the CURRENT value only (no max in the list)
Rows for a Digitama or a dexOnly / statless record draw no stat bars (gracefully absent, as the Dex block is when stats are nil)
The bars fit the existing narrow party row without clipping the name/stage/status already there
A test asserts the current-stat values resolved onto a PartyRow for a fixture Digimon, including a trained one so the bonus shows
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot of party rows showing the stat bars (reuse the -partyDemo launch flag)

userStories.title: Dex detail shows numeric HP/ATK/AGI values and the trainable maximum
userStories.description: As a player, I want the Dex detail stat bars to show actual numbers and how high each stat can go, so I know a Digimon's ceiling and not just an unlabelled bar. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-309.
acceptanceCriteria: StatBarsRow shows, per stat: the current value as a number and the maximum the Digimon can reach (base + trainingCap) — both readable, e.g. 'current / max' text plus a bar whose filled portion is current out of a total of max
The bar total is base + trainingCap (the ceiling) and the filled portion is the current value, so an untrained Digimon reads as partially filled toward its cap rather than solid full
Icons (heart/burst/hare) and per-stat tints are kept; VoiceOver reads 'Health: X of Y'-style labels reflecting current and max
DexStatBars (the no-view resolver) exposes both current and max so the numbers are testable without a screen; a test asserts current and max for a fixture stage (base vs base+cap)
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot of the Dex detail stat block showing numbers and the max

userStories.title: Nearby-battle transport: discover and connect two watches over the local network
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a peer-to-peer transport so two nearby watches can find each other and open a reliable connection without any backend server. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-310.
acceptanceCriteria: A transport type (e.g. NearbyBattleLink) uses Network.framework (NWListener + NWBrowser + NWConnection) with a Bonjour service type to advertise, browse, and connect one watch to another
Roles: one watch hosts (advertises + listens), the other joins (browses + connects); the API exposes discovered peers and a connect action
Connection state is observable (searching / connecting / connected / failed / disconnected) so the UI can reflect it; a failure or timeout surfaces a readable error rather than hanging

The required Info.plist keys for local networking + Bonjour (NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription, NSBonjourServices) are added via project.yml and xcodegen generate regenerates cleanly — the .xcodeproj is NOT hand-edited
Message framing is defined so a complete payload is delivered whole (length-prefixed or a documented delimiter), not assumed to arrive in one read
The transport is behind a protocol so it can be unit-tested with a fake/in-memory channel (no two real radios needed in a test)
Build green, tests pass for the framing/serialization and the observable state transitions over a fake channel

userStories.title: Deterministic stat exchange and shared bout resolution
userStories.description: As a player, when two connected watches battle, I want both to compute the same fight locally from each other's Digimon, so the result is fair and identical on both wrists with no server deciding it. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-311.
acceptanceCriteria: Each side serializes its combatant (stage, the stats/strengthStat/lifetime-energy the existing engine needs, element, display name, sprite ids) into a small Codable payload sent over the link (US-310)
A shared random seed is agreed deterministically (e.g. host sends it, or it is derived from both payloads) so both devices feed the SAME seed into the existing battle resolution

Both devices run the existing deterministic battle engine (the same code path pendingBattle / BattleView already uses) on (myCombatant, theirCombatant, seed) and produce the IDENTICAL BattleBout/report — a test asserts both sides' reports are equal for the same inputs
The existing BattleView replay is reused unchanged to show the fight
No network round-trip is needed DURING the replay — once both payloads + seed are exchanged, each device plays out the bout on its own
Build green, tests pass, including the both-sides-identical determinism test

userStories.title: Nearby-battle entry UI and connection flow
userStories.description: As a player, I want a screen to host or join a nearby battle, see the other player, connect, and drop into the fight. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-312.
acceptanceCriteria: A new entry point (reached from the Battle button or a dedicated control) offers Host and Join; Join lists discovered nearby peers with their Digimon's name
Selecting a peer / accepting a host connects (US-310), exchanges combatants + seed (US-311), then presents the shared BattleView replay over the game like the existing battle overlay
Connection status and errors are shown to the player (searching, connecting, failed/timeout, opponent left) with a way to cancel/back out cleanly
The nearby battle honours the same gates as a local battle where sensible (e.g. refused while the Digimon is an unhatched egg — isEgg)
The flow tears the link down when the battle ends or the screen is dismissed (no lingering advertiser/connection)
A view-model drives the flow and is tested using the fake channel from US-310; a DEBUG demo flag stages the host/join entry screen for a screenshot
Build green, tests pass. Verify in the simulator with a screenshot of the host/join entry screen

userStories.title: Record the outcome of a nearby battle
userStories.description: As a player, I want my wins and losses from nearby battles counted like any other battle, so my record reflects them. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-motion-layout-stats-and-nearby-battle.md, US-313.
acceptanceCriteria: When a nearby bout ends, the active Digimon's battleWins/battleLosses are updated exactly as a local battle updates them (same finishBattle bookkeeping path, or an equivalent that writes the same records)
A draw/disconnect mid-battle is handled by a documented rule (e.g. no record written on an aborted connection) and does not corrupt the save
The energy/charge cost of a nearby battle is consistent with the local battle's cost rules, or explicitly documented as free — pick one and state it in notes
A test asserts the win/loss record IS written for a resolved nearby bout and is NOT written for an aborted one
Build green, tests pass
